commit.message:
feat: support HH:MM:SS:MS timecode format in --timecodes flag (#5)

* feat: support HH:MM:SS:MS timecode format in --timecodes flag

Extends parseTimecodes to accept a four-part colon-separated format
(HH:MM:SS:MS) in addition to the existing HH:MM:SS[.nnn], Ns, and
NhNmNs formats. The MS component is scaled by digit count so that
e.g. :04 → 0.04s, :4 → 0.4s, :040 → 0.04s.

feat: initial keepassxc-browser-api library (#1)

* feat: initial keepassxc-browser-api library

- Migrate NaCl browser protocol client from keepassxc-ssh-agent
- Add BrowserConfig with shared config (~/.keepassxc/browser-api.json)
- Add Entry and Group dataclasses for API responses
- Add custom exceptions hierarchy (KeePassXCError base)
- Implement full browser API: get_logins, set_login, get_database_entries,
  get_database_groups, create_group, get_totp, delete_entry, lock_database,
  generate_password
- Cross-platform socket path (macOS + Linux/Flatpak)
- Full test suite (33 tests) + ruff linting
- CI workflows: lint_and_test, pypi, auto-release, auto-merge-dependabot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add request_autotype(); document unimplemented passkeys actions

- Add BrowserClient.request_autotype(search='') to wrap the
  request-autotype browser API action (triggers KeePassXC global auto-type)
- Add 3 tests for request_autotype
- Update README: add request_autotype to API table; note that
  passkeys-get and passkeys-register are not implemented (require
  complex WebAuthn structures and WITH_XC_BROWSER_PASSKEYS build flag)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add comprehensive PROTOCOL.md for KeePassXC browser extension API

Document the full wire protocol with references to the upstream KeePassXC
source files on GitHub (keepassxreboot/keepassxc):

- Transport: raw UTF-8 JSON on Unix socket, no length prefix, max 1MB
- clientID: required on every message; silent drop if absent
- Encryption: NaCl crypto_box_easy (Curve25519 + XSalsa20 + Poly1305)
- Nonce: client picks random 24-byte nonce; server increments by 1
- triggerUnlock: non-blocking; callers must poll until DB unlocks
- Error codes 1-33 with descriptions (from BrowserMessageBuilder.h)
- All 14 actions documented with request/response shapes:
  change-public-keys, associate, test-associate, get-databasehash,
  get-logins, set-login, get-database-entries, get-database-groups,
  create-new-group, get-totp, delete-entry, lock-database,
  generate-password, request-autotype
- Note on unimplemented passkeys-get / passkeys-register
- Typical session flow diagram

Also update README.md and CLAUDE.md to link to PROTOCOL.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: config race condition, auto-connect, generate_password API

- Fix config save race condition: use os.open with 0o600 + atomic rename
  to prevent secrets from being world-readable during write
- Add _ensure_session() for automatic connect + key exchange in API methods
- Add context manager support (__enter__/__exit__)
- Remove unused generate_password() parameters (KeePassXC uses its own
  configured password profile, client params were never sent)
- Update README with correct usage pattern and context manager example
- Add tests for context manager and _ensure_session

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: pin PyNaCl==1.6.2 for Dependabot tracking

Using >= prevents Dependabot from opening update PRs since any newer
version already satisfies the constraint. Pinning to == ensures
Dependabot detects new releases and triggers the auto-release chain
for keepassxc-browser-api itself.

* fix: remove spurious Notify-Homebrew job from pypi.yml

keepassxc-browser-api is a library with no Homebrew formula.
The dispatch event update-keepassxc-browser-api does not exist
in the homebrew-tap and would fail on every release.

commit.message:
fix(yjk,pkpm): restore engines and fix section/material mapping (#123)

* fix(yjk,pkpm): restore engines after upstream merge and fix section/material mapping

After the manifest-first refactor (#112), YJK and PKPM analysis engines
were silently dropped. This PR restores them and fixes several issues
found during review.

## Changes

### Engine restoration
- Add skill.yaml for yjk-static and pkpm-static (required by new
  manifest-first loader that replaced intent.md frontmatter)
- Add pkpm-static and yjk-static Python runtime files
- Restore builtin-yjk and builtin-pkpm in BUILTIN_ANALYSIS_ENGINES
- Add pkpm and yjk to BuiltInAnalysisEngineId, AnalysisRuntimeAdapterKey,
  and AnalysisSoftware types in types.ts
- Add pkpm and yjk to software enum in manifest-schema.ts

### YJK converter fixes (yjk_converter.py)
- Fix wrong kind values: box 4->7, L 6->28, T 5->29
- Expand _TYPE_TO_KIND from 7 to 30+ entries (all YJK 8.0 section kinds)
- Add _H_SECTION_DIMS lookup table with 30 GB/T 11263 H-sections for
  exact tw/tf instead of estimation formulas
- Fix box ShapeVal: H,B,T (3 params) -> B,H,U,T,D,F (6 params per SDK)
- Fix Floors_Assemb: iterate per story to support varying story heights
- Replace wildcard import with explicit: DataFunc, Hi_AddToAndReadYjk
- Add name arg and comments to fallback ColSect_Def/BeamSect_Def calls
- Document single-section API constraint on column_arrange/beam_arrange

### Material and model fixes
- runtime.py: replace hardcoded material name list with regex pattern
  (Q/S/A prefix for steel, C prefix for concrete)
- frame/model.ts: add sectionSubstitutions warning in metadata when
  user-specified section falls back to default

### Tests
- Update analysis-skill-registry test to include pkpm and yjk engines

* fix(yjk): remap V1 coordinates before story synthesis in _ensure_v2_model

V1 frame model uses y=vertical, z=plan-Y.
V2/YJK expects z=vertical, y=plan-Y.
Previously _ensure_v2_model read n.z for storey elevations, which is
always 0 for 2-D models and carries plan-depth for 3-D models  both
wrong.  Now we remap nodes first (V2.y=V1.z, V2.z=V1.y) and then
derive storey elevations from the remapped V2.z (vertical).

* fix(yjk,pkpm): address Copilot review comments on section schema alignment

runtime.py:
- Write standard_steel_name to both sec top-level (V2 canonical) and
  sec.properties (legacy compat) so downstream converters find it

yjk_converter.py:
- _build_shape_val: check sec['standard_steel_name'] first (V2 canonical),
  then fall back to properties/extra
- _TYPE_TO_KIND: add 'pipe' and 'hollow-circular' as aliases for kind=8

pkpm_converter.py:
- _KIND_MAP: add lowercase V2 schema aliases (box, tube, pipe,
  hollow-circular, rectangular, circular) alongside existing PascalCase
- _make_section_shape: read diameter from both 'D' and 'd' (V2 uses 'd')

* Update backend/src/agent-skills/analysis/yjk-static/yjk_converter.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(yjk): clarify element-type enrichment runs after coordinate remap

The Copilot review noted that dz-based column detection would
misclassify V1 frame members if z is not vertical.  The remap block
already runs before this section, so n['z'] is the vertical axis for
both V1 and native V2 payloads.  Add a comment to make the ordering
explicit and prevent future confusion.

* fix(analysis): add intent stage to pkpm-static and yjk-static skill manifests

All other analysis skills include both intent and analysis stages.

commit.message:
fix: auto-close implemented issues on merge (#72)

## Problem

Generated implement PRs use `Implements #N` in the PR body. GitHub does
not treat `Implements` as an auto-closing keyword, so completed issues
stay open after the PR merges.

## Solution

Switch the generated PR body to `Closes #N` so issue state tracks merged
implementations automatically.

## Changes

- update `implement_issue()` PR body wording from `Implements` to
`Closes`
- add a regression test asserting the generated PR body contains the
auto-closing keyword

fix: satoshi slot hardening — lock contention, key export, zeroization

Critical fixes from audit of 680a445 (feat: satoshi slot):

Lock contention: Restructured bitcoin_service_task batch loop to hold
mutex only for short read/write of shared state. ECC point multiplication
(16 × 2 calls, 300-500ms) now runs outside the lock. Replaced
slot_process_candidate_locked with pure slot_evaluate_candidate that
returns results via stack struct.

Private key export (was completely missing): Added NVS read-back
(slot_read_hit_record), Base58Check/WIF encoding (slot_encode_wif),
public API (bitcoin_service_read_hit_record), device_link RPC
(slot.export_hit), and esp32dash CLI command (slot export).

Zeroization: Applied mbedtls_platform_zeroize to sha256/pubkey in
hash160 computation, candidate buffer after batch, hit_record after
NVS write, hit_privkey/result after applying results, and WIF encoding
intermediates.

Also fixed: cJSON leak in Binance feed early return, deduplicated
scale_double/scale_positive_double into market_http shared module.

commit.message:
feat: add -readrate to per-track ffmpeg for near-realtime output

Instead of modifying MA server's -re flag globally (which would break
Spotify Connect and other realtime plugin sources), throttle our own
per-track ffmpeg input reading to ~1.1x with a 5-second initial burst.

This makes our PCM output arrive at near-realtime rate, compatible
with MA's -re flag on the outer ffmpeg, while eliminating startup
latency via the initial burst.

Applied to both _stream_track and prebuffer _fill paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
